checks.title: No real email addresses in source
checks.description: Source files must not contain real email addresses. Use @example.test for mock data.

checks.title: No real phone numbers in source
checks.description: Source files should not contain patterns that look like real phone numbers.

checks.title: No SSN patterns in source
checks.description: Source files must not contain social security number patterns.

checks.title: Manifest has a slug
checks.description: plannerxchange.app.json must define a non-empty slug.

checks.title: Manifest has a name
checks.description: plannerxchange.app.json must define a non-empty name.

checks.title: Manifest has an entryPoint
checks.description: plannerxchange.app.json must define an entryPoint.

checks.title: Manifest has permissions
checks.description: plannerxchange.app.json should list required permission scopes.

checks.title: Build output exists
checks.description: dist/ directory must exist. Run `npm run build` before preflight.

checks.title: Publish manifest generated
checks.description: dist/plannerxchange.publish.json must exist after build.

checks.title: Build provenance generated
checks.description: dist/plannerxchange.build-provenance.json must exist after build.

checks.title: CodeQL workflow exists
checks.description: PlannerXchange requires GitHub CodeQL code-scanning evidence for the linked branch commit.

checks.title: Browser storage usage flagged
checks.description: Browser storage calls detected. Ensure localStorage, sessionStorage, and IndexedDB are mock-only or UI-preference-only and not used for production data persistence or PII.

checks.title: No appInstallationId query parameter
checks.description: Installed-app context must travel through shell-managed authenticatedFetch and x-plannerxchange-app-installation-id, not query strings.

checks.title: Manual auth header usage flagged
checks.description: Hosted apps should not read, store, or manually send PlannerXchange bearer tokens. Use ShellRuntimeContext.authenticatedFetch for PX API calls.

checks.title: No direct KMS or decrypt access
checks.description: Builder apps must not include direct KMS clients, decrypt commands, or restricted-PII decrypt helpers. PlannerXchange performs decryption only inside governed backend APIs.

checks.title: No builder-owned data backend for PX/client data
checks.description: Shell-published self-serve apps must not use Neon, Supabase, Firebase, Prisma, Postgres, MongoDB, Redis, database URL env vars, or service-role keys for PX/client/subscriber data.

checks.title: CSV/file ingress must be declared
checks.description: File inputs, FileReader, FormData uploads, Papa Parse, csv/xlsx packages, and drag/drop uploads require dataIngressDeclarations and review of the target storage lane.

checks.title: No shell-only import or integration routes
checks.description: Builder apps must not call /imports/*, /integrations/*, Core Data mapping/validation/execute routes, or shell-owned provider routes directly.

checks.title: No external CSV/file upload hosts
checks.description: Apps that handle PX/client CSV or file uploads must use PlannerXchange-controlled ingress or enterprise exception review, not vendor upload endpoints.

checks.title: External AI SDK usage flagged
checks.description: External AI-provider usage is a publish-review trigger. Day 1 external AI-provider or third-party egress of PX client data is not allowed.

checks.title: No hardcoded PX API URLs
checks.description: Use ctx.apiBaseUrl from the shell runtime context instead of hardcoding API URLs. This ensures apps work correctly across dev/staging/prod environments.
